threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest tube station without realizing none are close enough to bother.
Heard a bloke on the 59 last night claiming he could predict the future based on the bus routes. Then he pointed out the wrong stop twice.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the kid at the Kiosk knew my order before I walked in. That felt like home, like I was part of the neighborhood's rhythm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a club that operates in a former factory, complete with a swimming pool. It's wild and feels like stepping into a time capsule.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows to avoid the Civic Center BART during rush hour, especially with all the recent delays and the dog's still-on-the-tracks antics.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a couple arguing loudly on the bus, mid-sentence he stopped and handed her a slice of pizza. Only in SF do disputes come with a side of carbs.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for selfies; they miss the real vibe in the quiet backstreets, where locals sip coffee and debate fútbol.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they stare at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, trying to decipher the lines and stops.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the tube' instead of 'the Overground' and look lost when the bus is late. Welcome to the real Hackney, mate.
Caught the 59 last night, driver stopped for a guy selling homemade kebabs at 2 AM. Only in Brixton, I suppose.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One morning, I stopped at my local bakery and saw the owner brewing coffee for a couple of Turkish grandmas discussing politics. That's when it hit me; this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a late-night Späti serving currywurst and craft beer, while debating the latest techno tracks with the cashier. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot locals by how they casually avoid eye contact with the guy yelling about the robot uprising on 16th street.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a full tuxedo playing violin on a packed Muni bus, completely unfazed by the chaos of the Civic Center delays. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think all the locals are at the plaça for the paella, but half the crowd is just waiting for the vermut to kick in while discussing Barça.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still gawking at the colorful murals, taking snapshots of everything while awkwardly trying to decipher the metro map.
